zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard Burr於1495年出生在英國艾可格亞特 坎菲爾德時，他的父親Andrew Bures18歲，母親Alice Smythe15歲。他於1517年與Agnes DeBishops在英國艾可格亞特 坎菲爾德結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和4個女兒的父母。他去世於1569年12月11日在英國英格蘭艾可格亞特 坎菲爾德享年74歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭倫敦。

nameMeanings.name: Burr
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: nickname from Middle English burre ‘bur’ (a seed-case or flower-head with clinging prickles), used by Shakespeare to denote someone who sticks like a bur, a person difficult to ‘shake off’, a sense which may well be older.
German: topographic name from Burr(e) ‘mound, hill’, or in the south a variant of Burrer .

History: The American political leader Aaron Burr (1756–1836) was the son of a clergyman and academic, president of Princeton University. On his mother's side he was descended from the Puritan preacher Jonathan Edwards; on his father's from Jehu Burr, who emigrated from England to MA with John Winthrop (see   Winthrop  ) in 1630.

variants.name: Burrer
meanings: from an agent derivative of Middle High German burren ‘to buzz, complain’, probably applied as a nickname for a noisy or cantankerous person.
habitational name for someone from either of two places called Burren in Württemberg.

variants.name: Burrs
meanings: Altered form of English Burr , with the addition of excrescent -s, or perhaps of Burrows .

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

en.lifeSummary: When Richard Burr was born in 1495, in Great Canfield, Essex, England, his father, Andrew Bures, was 18 and his mother, Alice Smythe, was 15. He married Agnes DeBishops in 1517, in Great Canfield, Essex, England. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 4 daughters. He died on 11 December 1569, in Great Canfield, Essex, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 74, and was buried in London, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Richard Burr est né en 1495, à Great Canfield, Essex, Angleterre, son père, Andrew Bures, avait 18 ans et sa mère, Alice Smythe, avait 15 ans. Il a épousé Agnes DeBishops en 1517, à Great Canfield, Essex, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils et 4 filles. Il est décédé le 11 décembre 1569, à Great Canfield, Essex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 74 ans, et a été enterré à Londres, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

ja.lifeSummary: Richard Burrが1495年にイギリスエセックスグレアト カンフィールドで生まれた時，父Andrew Buresは18歳，母Alice Smytheは15歳であった。1517年にイギリスエセックスグレアト カンフィールドでAgnes DeBishopsと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼は1569年12月11日にイギリスイギリスエセックスグレアト カンフィールドで74歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスロンドンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Richard Burr(이)가 1495년 영국 에세스 그리트 캔필드에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Andrew Bures(은)는 18세였고, 그의 어머니 Alice Smythe(은)는 15세였다. 그는 1517년 영국 에세스 그리트 캔필드에서 Agnes DeBishops(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1569년 12월 11일 영국 잉글랜드 에세스 그리트 캔필드에서 74세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 런던에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Richard Burr nasceu em 1495, em Great Canfield, Essex, Inglaterra, seu pai, Andrew Bures, tinha 18 anos e sua mãe, Alice Smythe, tinha 15 anos. Ele casou-se com Agnes DeBishops em 1517, em Great Canfield, Essex, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 6 filhos e 4 filhas. Ele faleceu em 11 de dezembro de 1569, em Great Canfield, Essex, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 74 anos, e foi sepultado em London, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard Burr родился в 1495 г. в местности грит канфиелд, эссекс, Англия, его отцу по имени Andrew Bures было 18 лет, а матери, которую звали Alice Smythe, 15. Он женился на женщине по имени Agnes DeBishops в 1517 г. в местности грит канфиелд, эссекс, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 4 дочери. Он умер 11 декабря 1569 г. в местности грит канфиелд, эссекс, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 74 лет и был похоронен в местности Лондон, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Richard Burr nació en 1495, en Great Canfield, Essex, Inglaterra, su papá, Andrew Bures, tenía 18 años y su mamá, Alice Smythe, tenía 15 años. Se casó con Agnes DeBishops en 1517, en Great Canfield, Essex, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 6 hijos y 4 hijas. Murió el 11 de diciembre de 1569, en Great Canfield, Essex, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 74 años, y fue sepultado en London, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
